surfaces.title: Tensor contracts and typed refusal vocabulary
surfaces.current_truth: typed tensor ids, dtypes, shapes, devices, quantization layouts, and refusal codes already exist as shared engine contracts
surfaces.extension_or_gap: no article-specific closure gap lives here; higher layers consume these contracts

surfaces.title: Lazy array facade and bounded public materialization surface
surfaces.current_truth: the repo already has a bounded graph-backed array surface with explicit evaluation, backend identities, cache posture, and debug capture hooks
surfaces.extension_or_gap: the owned article-equivalence stack still needs broader transformer-oriented tensor/view/math coverage than the current narrow public array surface provides

surfaces.title: Module state tree and basic reusable layers
surfaces.current_truth: module trees, parameter traversal, quantized wrappers, and reusable Linear/Embedding/LayerNorm/RmsNorm/Dropout layers already exist as the lower-level layer substrate
surfaces.extension_or_gap: the lower-level layer substrate now exists at the right boundary; the remaining gap is the paper-faithful article model and proof route above it, not missing primitive NN layers

surfaces.title: Canonical reusable Transformer-boundary configs
surfaces.current_truth: the dedicated `psionic-transformer` crate now owns reusable decoder and AttnRes architecture primitives plus the owned scaled dot-product attention, embeddings, encoder-decoder stack, feed-forward, residual, and norm path at the intended layering boundary
surfaces.extension_or_gap: the remaining gap is now the article-specific vocabulary, artifact, training, and proof route on top of this boundary, not missing reusable Transformer architecture

surfaces.title: Model descriptors and weight-lineage metadata
surfaces.current_truth: shared model descriptors, weight bundle metadata, and artifact-governance wrappers already exist for reusable model families
surfaces.extension_or_gap: these surfaces can be reused directly once the canonical article model artifact exists

surfaces.title: Canonical article Transformer wrapper
surfaces.current_truth: the repo now has one canonical paper-faithful article wrapper that binds the owned encoder-decoder stack, paper reference, and embedding-sharing modes at the `psionic-models` boundary
surfaces.extension_or_gap: the wrapper still needs article trace vocabulary, artifact-backed weights, lineage, and proof-route closure before the final article-equivalence route can turn green

surfaces.title: AttnRes reference model family
surfaces.current_truth: the repo already has a bounded CPU-reference AttnRes family that is useful as a model-architecture pilot and training/control experiment surface
surfaces.extension_or_gap: it is not the canonical article route and remains a research-only forcing function rather than a closure blocker

surfaces.title: Existing executor-transformer scaffold
surfaces.current_truth: the repo already has executor-transformer configs, weight bundles, forward-pass scaffolding, decode state, KV points, and refusal posture
surfaces.extension_or_gap: the scaffold is no longer the canonical article route; it remains a separate research and comparison lane beside the paper-faithful article wrapper

surfaces.title: Fixture-backed article executor lane
surfaces.current_truth: the current fixture-backed article executor lane still carries the bounded Rust-only article closeout and compatibility truth
surfaces.extension_or_gap: canonical article-equivalence closure cannot stop at this reference-fixture lane; it must graduate to an owned Transformer-backed artifact

surfaces.title: Runtime proof identity and trace receipts
surfaces.current_truth: execution proof bundles, runtime identity, trace ABI contracts, trace artifacts, and replay-safe evidence surfaces already exist
surfaces.extension_or_gap: these runtime truth surfaces can be reused directly once the owned article route emits the right artifacts

surfaces.title: Article-closeout runtime and direct-proof receipts
surfaces.current_truth: the runtime already owns article ABI, article runtime closeout, and direct model-weight proof surfaces
surfaces.extension_or_gap: those receipts still need rebinding from the current bounded lanes into the canonical owned Transformer-backed article route

existing_substrate_inventory_report.current_truth_boundary: psionic already owns real reusable substrate for tensor contracts, bounded array execution, module state, basic layers, transformer-boundary configs, model descriptors, weight lineage metadata, runtime proof identity, and trace receipts. The current gap is not substrate absence; it is the missing owned article-equivalence stack and the current presence of bounded research or fixture-backed lanes that are not yet sufficient for canonical article closure.
existing_substrate_inventory_report.claim_boundary: this report inventories reusable substrate only. It freezes what can be reused, what needs extension, what stays research-only, and what is still not sufficient for article closure. It does not claim that the owned article-equivalence Transformer stack, the canonical article model artifact, or the final article-equivalence proof route already exist.
existing_substrate_inventory_report.summary: Existing substrate inventory now records surface_count=11, blocker_surface_count=6, non_blocker_surface_count=5, inventory_contract_green=true, tied_requirement_satisfied=true, and article_equivalence_green=true.

interface_rows.detail: tensor metadata, device/layout truth, and bounded array materialization stay below the canonical article route and are reused through `psionic-core` plus `psionic-array`

module state, primitive layers, and reusable transformer attention plus encoder-decoder composition stay split between `psionic-nn` and `psionic-transformer`, with `psionic-transformer` as the architecture anchor
canonical article-model descriptors, canonical route selection, and later artifact identity stay owned by `psionic-models` and must consume the `psionic-transformer` boundary rather than bypass it
forward-pass trace hooks stay model-owned at the article-model boundary and serialize into runtime-owned trace ABI surfaces instead of inventing a second trace layer
replay, proof, trace receipt, and runtime identity hooks stay owned by `psionic-runtime` as the canonical receipt boundary for the article route

ownership_rows.ownership_role: tensor ids, dtypes, shapes, devices, layouts, and typed refusals
bounded array materialization, graph-backed arithmetic, and public array runtime surface
module state plus reusable primitive layer implementations

canonical reusable transformer architecture boundary plus owned attention, embeddings, encoder-decoder stack composition, feed-forward, residual, and norm logic above primitive layers and below model artifacts
canonical article-route model wrapper, config, route descriptor, and forward-pass surface owner
runtime proof identity, replay, trace receipt, and execution challenge boundary

ownership_diagram_lines: psionic-models -> canonical article model artifact + forward-pass hooks
psionic-runtime -> replay, trace, and receipt hooks

dependency_checks.detail: `psionic-models` must depend on `psionic-transformer` so the canonical article model artifact cannot bypass the architecture boundary
`psionic-transformer` must depend on `psionic-nn` so reusable block composition stays above the layer substrate instead of rebuilding private layer logic
`psionic-transformer` must stay below `psionic-models` and `psionic-runtime` so the architecture boundary remains reusable and not product-coupled
`psionic-nn` must stay below training, model, and runtime crates so it can act as the lower-level layer and module substrate for the canonical transformer route
`psionic-runtime` must not depend on `psionic-models`; replay and receipt hooks stay runtime-owned and model-agnostic
`psionic-models` must stay below eval and research crates so the canonical article route uses real runtime/model modules rather than review-only layers

canonical_boundary_report.claim_boundary: this report freezes one explicit canonical Transformer stack boundary for the article-equivalence closure wave. The route is not one monolithic crate; it is one required boundary spanning existing crates with `psionic-transformer` as the architecture anchor, `psionic-models` as the article-model and artifact owner, and `psionic-runtime` as the replay/receipt owner. This does not claim the final article-equivalence stack is complete or green.
canonical_boundary_report.summary: Canonical Transformer stack boundary now records interface_rows=5, dependency_checks=6, tied_requirement_satisfied=true, boundary_contract_green=true, and article_equivalence_green=true.

envelope_report.claim_boundary: this report closes TAS-179 only. It freezes one declared article interpreter breadth envelope over the frozen core-Wasm floor, the current named article i32 profiles, and the later required search-process, long-horizon control, and module-scale Wasm-loop families while keeping linked-program bundles research-only and import-mediated processes, dynamic-memory resume, memory64, multi-memory, component-linking, exception profiles, and broader float semantics explicitly outside the article envelope. It does not yet build the breadth suite or turn final article-equivalence green.
envelope_report.summary: Article interpreter breadth envelope report now records tied_requirement_satisfied=true, current_floor_green=2/2, declared_required_family_green=3/3, research_only_family_green=1/1, explicit_out_of_envelope_green=7/7, envelope_contract_green=true, and article_equivalence_green=true.

claim_boundary: this report closes TAS-184 only. It binds the owned Transformer route, the generic article workload-family direct no-tool proof suite, the already-green breadth/generalization/independence/no-spill prerequisites, and concrete weight-perturbation sensitivity into one clean-room interpreter-ownership verdict. By itself it still does not replace the later KV-cache and activation-state discipline verdict, cross-machine reproducibility matrix, route-minimality audit, or final article-equivalence audit.
summary: Article interpreter-ownership gate now records tied_requirement_satisfied=true, generic_direct_proof_cases=6, breadth_families_green=8/8, route_purity_green=true, perturbation_sensitivity_green=true, ownership_gate_green=true, blocked_issue_frontier=None, and article_equivalence_green=true.
